meta_title: For Students: freelance pitching to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to freelance pitching and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Freelance Pitching to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no portfolio' trap by mastering freelance pitching. A high-level guide for students using Antigravity and Claude Code to secure your first client.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for experience and start engineering it. By identifying high-leverage pain points in local businesses and using Claude Code to build rapid, functional prototypes, you bypass the need for a legacy portfolio. Pitch the solution, not the resume, and secure your first paid project through high-velocity technical delivery.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank repository, the 'no experience' paradox haunting every job application. The industry demands a portfolio you haven't built yet, and the cycle feels unbreakable. You have the technical hunger, but you lack the battle scars that clients demand before they trust you with their capital.

But here is the secret: clients don't care about your resume; they care about their problems. We are going to flip the script. Instead of begging for a job, you will use Antigravity and Claude Code to build a 'Proof of Value'—a surgical technical solution that solves a specific business pain point—and pitch it as a high-value freelance engagement.

steps.title: Identify a High-Value Niche Problem
steps.description: Find a local business with a clear technical inefficiency, such as manual data entry or a broken booking flow. Your goal is to find a friction point that can be solved in under 48 hours.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this business website [URL] and identify three technical bottlenecks that prevent customer conversion. Provide a list of features that could solve these in a single weekend.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'research [URL] and identify 3 key technical pain points for conversion optimization'

steps.title: Architect the Proof of Value
steps.description: Use Antigravity to structure the MVP architecture. Don't build the whole app; build the 'magic moment' that proves you can solve their specific problem.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a high-level architecture diagram for a [Feature Name] MVP. Focus on low-latency, serverless implementation using Supabase and Vercel.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate project scaffolding for [Feature Name] using Next.js and Tailwind'

steps.title: Rapid Iteration with Claude Code
steps.description: Use Claude Code to automate the boilerplate and integrate the core logic. This ensures your code is production-grade, even if you are just starting.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this code to handle edge cases in the booking logic and ensure 99% uptime for the API routes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement core logic for [Feature Name] and write unit tests for edge cases'

steps.title: Deploy the 'Demo' Environment
steps.description: Deploy your prototype to a live URL. Nothing sells better than a working link that a prospect can click immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide the best practices for deploying a secure, public-facing demo of this app to Vercel with environment variable protection.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'configure deployment pipeline and secure environment variables for production'

steps.title: The Value-First Pitch
steps.description: Email the business owner with a short video of the tool working. Focus on the ROI (time/money saved) rather than the code you wrote.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a cold outreach email that highlights the specific problem I solved for [Business Name] and offers a 15-minute demo.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'draft a professional cold email template for technical consulting outreach'

pro_tips: Never send a resume; send a working link to a solution that solves one specific pain point.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your demo; it signals to clients that you prioritize reliability over 'hacker' code.
Time-box your prototype development to 12 hours; if it takes longer, you are over-engineering and losing focus on the pitch.
When pitching, ask for a 'pilot project' rather than a long-term contract to lower the barrier to entry.
Document your build process in a blog post while you code; this becomes your 'de facto' portfolio for future clients.
content.next_steps: Once your first client accepts the pilot, use the feedback to iterate the tool into a standalone SaaS product.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for permission to be a professional. Use these tools to prove your worth, secure your first paycheck, and launch your engineering career. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
